verificationNote: Control IDs and domain structure from CSA CCM v4.0.10 (publicly available). Descriptions paraphrased. Obtain the normative spreadsheet from cloudsecurityalliance.org for authoritative control text.

controlDomains.name: Application & Interface Security
controlDomains.description: Controls for securing cloud applications and their interfaces throughout the development lifecycle, including secure coding practices, API security, and application vulnerability management.

exampleControls.name: Application Security Policy
exampleControls.summary: Establish and maintain an application security policy defining requirements for secure development, testing, and deployment of cloud applications.

exampleControls.name: Application Security Baseline
exampleControls.summary: Define and enforce a security baseline for all cloud applications that specifies minimum security requirements and hardening standards.

exampleControls.name: Secure Application Design and Development
exampleControls.summary: Implement secure-by-design principles and secure coding practices during the application development lifecycle, including threat modelling and code review.

exampleControls.name: Automated Application Security Testing
exampleControls.summary: Implement automated security testing (SAST, DAST, SCA) integrated into CI/CD pipelines to detect vulnerabilities before deployment.

controlDomains.name: Audit & Assurance
controlDomains.description: Controls for conducting independent security audits, managing audit scope and planning, and maintaining audit evidence to provide assurance of control effectiveness.

exampleControls.name: Audit & Assurance Policy
exampleControls.summary: Establish and maintain an audit policy defining scope, frequency, independence requirements, and reporting obligations for cloud security audits.

exampleControls.name: Independent Assessments
exampleControls.summary: Conduct independent security assessments at planned intervals or upon significant changes to validate control effectiveness.

exampleControls.name: Risk-Based Planning Assessment
exampleControls.summary: Plan audit activities based on risk assessment results, prioritising high-risk areas and ensuring adequate coverage of critical controls.

controlDomains.name: Business Continuity Management & Operational Resilience
controlDomains.description: Controls for ensuring cloud service continuity, disaster recovery, backup management, and operational resilience across all service models and deployment scenarios.

exampleControls.name: Business Continuity Planning
exampleControls.summary: Establish and maintain a business continuity plan covering cloud service disruptions, including RTOs, RPOs, and recovery procedures for all critical cloud services.

exampleControls.name: Business Continuity Testing
exampleControls.summary: Test business continuity and disaster recovery plans at planned intervals, documenting results and updating plans based on lessons learned.

exampleControls.name: Backup
exampleControls.summary: Implement backup procedures for cloud-hosted data and configurations with defined retention periods, encryption requirements, and recovery testing.

exampleControls.name: Equipment Redundancy
exampleControls.summary: Implement redundant infrastructure components to eliminate single points of failure in cloud service delivery.

controlDomains.name: Change Control & Configuration Management
controlDomains.description: Controls for managing changes to cloud infrastructure, applications, and configurations through formal change management processes, baseline configurations, and unauthorised change detection.

exampleControls.name: Change Management Policy
exampleControls.summary: Establish and maintain a change management policy governing all changes to cloud infrastructure, platforms, and applications, including emergency change procedures.

exampleControls.name: Quality Testing
exampleControls.summary: Perform quality and security testing on all changes before deployment to production cloud environments.

exampleControls.name: Change Agreements
exampleControls.summary: Define formal agreements with cloud service providers specifying notification requirements and approval processes for changes that may affect consumers.

exampleControls.name: Change Restoration
exampleControls.summary: Maintain the ability to restore cloud services to a known-good state following failed or unauthorized changes, including automated rollback capabilities.

controlDomains.name: Cryptography, Encryption & Key Management
controlDomains.description: Controls for implementing cryptographic protections for data at rest, in transit, and in use within cloud environments, including encryption algorithm selection, key lifecycle management, and certificate management.

exampleControls.name: Encryption & Key Management Policy
exampleControls.summary: Establish and maintain a cryptography policy defining approved algorithms, key lengths, key management procedures, and certificate lifecycle requirements.

exampleControls.name: Data Encryption
exampleControls.summary: Encrypt sensitive data at rest and in transit using industry-standard algorithms and key lengths appropriate to data classification.

exampleControls.name: Encryption Change Management
exampleControls.summary: Manage cryptographic changes through formal change control to prevent service disruption and maintain data protection continuity.

exampleControls.name: Key Generation
exampleControls.summary: Generate cryptographic keys using approved random number generators with sufficient entropy, in accordance with industry standards and regulatory requirements.

controlDomains.name: Data Security & Privacy Lifecycle Management
controlDomains.description: Controls for protecting data throughout its lifecycle in cloud environments — from classification and creation through storage, use, sharing, and secure disposal. Includes privacy controls for personal data processing.

exampleControls.name: Security & Privacy Policy
exampleControls.summary: Establish and maintain a data security and privacy policy addressing data classification, handling requirements, and privacy obligations across all cloud deployments.

exampleControls.name: Data Classification
exampleControls.summary: Classify data according to sensitivity and criticality to determine appropriate protection controls and handling requirements in cloud environments.

exampleControls.name: Data Protection by Design and Default
exampleControls.summary: Implement data protection measures by design and by default in all cloud applications and services, minimising data collection and applying purpose limitation.

exampleControls.name: Data Protection Impact Assessment
exampleControls.summary: Conduct data protection impact assessments for cloud processing activities that present high risk to individuals, particularly for cross-border data transfers.

controlDomains.name: Datacenter Security
controlDomains.description: Controls for physical security of cloud datacenter facilities, including access controls, environmental protections, equipment security, and secure media handling.

exampleControls.name: Datacenter Security Policy
exampleControls.summary: Establish and maintain physical security policies for all datacenter facilities hosting cloud services, defining access zones and protection requirements.

exampleControls.name: Controlled Access Points
exampleControls.summary: Implement controlled access points with multi-factor authentication, visitor management, and audit logging for all datacenter entry points.

exampleControls.name: Secure Area Authorization
exampleControls.summary: Restrict access to sensitive areas within the datacenter (server rooms, network rooms, storage areas) to authorised personnel with documented business need.

exampleControls.name: Equipment Identification
exampleControls.summary: Uniquely identify and track all datacenter equipment assets, maintaining an up-to-date inventory with location and custodian information.

controlDomains.name: Governance, Risk Management & Compliance
controlDomains.description: Controls for establishing cloud security governance structures, risk management processes, regulatory compliance tracking, and information security management systems for cloud environments.

exampleControls.name: Information Security Program
exampleControls.summary: Establish and maintain a cloud security programme aligned with business objectives, regulatory requirements, and industry standards, with executive sponsorship and adequate resourcing.

exampleControls.name: Risk Management Program
exampleControls.summary: Implement a formal risk management programme covering cloud-specific risks including shared responsibility gaps, vendor lock-in, data sovereignty, and multi-tenancy.

exampleControls.name: Organisational Policy
exampleControls.summary: Define and maintain organisational policies governing cloud adoption, usage, and security requirements across all business units.

exampleControls.name: Information System Regulatory Mapping
exampleControls.summary: Map cloud information systems to applicable regulatory and contractual obligations to ensure comprehensive compliance coverage.

controlDomains.name: Human Resources Security
controlDomains.description: Controls for managing human resource security risks throughout the employment lifecycle — screening, onboarding, awareness training, role changes, and termination — for personnel with access to cloud services.

exampleControls.name: Background Screening Policy
exampleControls.summary: Screen all personnel with access to cloud infrastructure and customer data according to applicable laws, regulations, and risk level of their role.

exampleControls.name: Acceptable Use of Technology Policy
exampleControls.summary: Define and communicate acceptable use policies for cloud technologies, devices, and data, including personal device usage and shadow IT restrictions.

exampleControls.name: Security Awareness Training
exampleControls.summary: Provide role-appropriate cloud security awareness training upon hire and at planned intervals, covering cloud-specific threats such as credential phishing, misconfiguration risks, and data leakage.

exampleControls.name: Return of Assets
exampleControls.summary: Ensure return or secure destruction of all cloud access credentials, tokens, and assets upon employment termination or role change.

controlDomains.name: Identity & Access Management
controlDomains.description: Controls for managing digital identities and access to cloud resources, including authentication, authorisation, privilege management, federation, and access reviews across cloud environments.

exampleControls.name: Identity & Access Management Policy
exampleControls.summary: Establish and maintain an identity and access management policy defining requirements for authentication, authorisation, federation, and privilege management in cloud environments.

exampleControls.name: Strong Password Policy
exampleControls.summary: Enforce strong password policies including minimum complexity, length, history, and lockout thresholds for all cloud accounts.

exampleControls.name: Separation of Duties
exampleControls.summary: Implement separation of duties in cloud environments to prevent any single individual from having excessive access that could lead to fraud or error.

exampleControls.name: Management of Privileged Access
exampleControls.summary: Implement enhanced controls for privileged cloud accounts including just-in-time access, session recording, multi-factor authentication, and regular access certification.

controlDomains.name: Interoperability & Portability
controlDomains.description: Controls for ensuring cloud service interoperability and data portability, enabling organisations to migrate between cloud providers or return to on-premises infrastructure without vendor lock-in.

exampleControls.name: Interoperability & Portability Policy
exampleControls.summary: Establish and maintain policies requiring cloud services to support data portability and service interoperability using open standards where available.

exampleControls.name: Application Portability
exampleControls.summary: Design cloud applications using portable architectures (containers, abstraction layers, standard APIs) to minimise vendor lock-in and enable migration.

exampleControls.name: Data Portability
exampleControls.summary: Ensure data stored in cloud services can be exported in standard, machine-readable formats with complete fidelity and within agreed timeframes.

exampleControls.name: Data Location
exampleControls.summary: Define and enforce data residency requirements specifying where cloud data may be stored and processed, based on regulatory, contractual, and organisational requirements.

controlDomains.name: Infrastructure & Virtualization Security
controlDomains.description: Controls for securing cloud infrastructure components including hypervisors, virtual machines, containers, virtual networks, and storage systems. Covers network segmentation, hardening, and capacity management.

exampleControls.name: Infrastructure & Virtualization Security Policy
exampleControls.summary: Establish and maintain security policies for cloud infrastructure covering hypervisor hardening, VM isolation, container security, and virtual network segmentation.

exampleControls.name: Network Security
exampleControls.summary: Implement network security controls including virtual firewalls, security groups, network ACLs, and micro-segmentation to restrict traffic between cloud workloads.

exampleControls.name: Segmentation and Segregation
exampleControls.summary: Segment cloud environments by sensitivity level, workload type, and tenant boundary using virtual network isolation, separate accounts/subscriptions, and landing zone architectures.

exampleControls.name: Operating System Hardening
exampleControls.summary: Harden operating systems deployed in cloud environments using industry benchmarks (CIS Benchmarks), removing unnecessary services, and applying security configurations.

controlDomains.name: Logging & Monitoring
controlDomains.description: Controls for implementing comprehensive logging, monitoring, and alerting across cloud environments, including audit trail protection, log aggregation, security event correlation, and clock synchronisation.

exampleControls.name: Logging & Monitoring Policy
exampleControls.summary: Establish and maintain a logging policy defining what events to log, retention periods, protection requirements, and monitoring responsibilities across cloud services.

exampleControls.name: Security Monitoring & Alerting
exampleControls.summary: Implement security monitoring and alerting for cloud environments, including intrusion detection, anomaly detection, and automated response capabilities.

exampleControls.name: Audit Log Protection
exampleControls.summary: Protect audit logs from unauthorized access, modification, and deletion using immutable storage, separate accounts, and integrity verification.

exampleControls.name: Failures & Anomalies Reporting
exampleControls.summary: Detect, report, and investigate failures and anomalies in cloud logging systems to ensure continuous monitoring coverage.

controlDomains.name: Security Incident Management, E-Discovery & Cloud Forensics
controlDomains.description: Controls for cloud security incident management including incident response planning, evidence collection, chain of custody, e-discovery support, and cloud-specific forensic investigation procedures.

exampleControls.name: Security Incident Management Policy
exampleControls.summary: Establish and maintain a security incident management policy covering cloud-specific scenarios including cross-tenant incidents, provider notification obligations, and shared incident response responsibilities.

exampleControls.name: Service Management Policy
exampleControls.summary: Define service management policies covering incident classification, escalation procedures, SLA requirements, and coordination between cloud provider and consumer during security incidents.

exampleControls.name: Incident Response Testing
exampleControls.summary: Test cloud incident response plans through tabletop exercises and simulations at planned intervals, including scenarios involving cloud provider cooperation and cross-border data handling.

exampleControls.name: Evidence Collection & Forensics
exampleControls.summary: Define procedures for collecting, preserving, and analysing digital evidence in cloud environments, addressing ephemeral resources, shared infrastructure, and multi-jurisdictional legal requirements.

controlDomains.name: Supply Chain Management, Transparency & Accountability
controlDomains.description: Controls for managing cloud supply chain risks including sub-processor oversight, vendor due diligence, transparency of operations, and accountability across the cloud service delivery chain.

exampleControls.name: Supply Chain Management Policy
exampleControls.summary: Establish and maintain a supply chain management policy addressing cloud service provider selection, sub-processor management, and third-party risk assessment requirements.

exampleControls.name: Sub-Processor Management
exampleControls.summary: Maintain visibility and control over cloud sub-processors (fourth-party providers), including notification of changes, security assessment requirements, and contractual flow-down obligations.

exampleControls.name: Supply Chain Inventory
exampleControls.summary: Maintain an up-to-date inventory of all cloud supply chain components including service providers, sub-processors, open source dependencies, and third-party integrations.

exampleControls.name: Supply Chain Data Security Assessment
exampleControls.summary: Assess data security practices of cloud supply chain participants to verify they meet organisational and regulatory data protection requirements.

controlDomains.name: Threat & Vulnerability Management
controlDomains.description: Controls for identifying, assessing, and remediating threats and vulnerabilities in cloud environments, including vulnerability scanning, penetration testing, threat intelligence integration, and patch management.

exampleControls.name: Threat & Vulnerability Management Policy
exampleControls.summary: Establish and maintain a threat and vulnerability management policy defining scanning frequency, remediation SLAs, exception processes, and responsibility allocation between provider and consumer.

exampleControls.name: Vulnerability Identification
exampleControls.summary: Implement automated vulnerability scanning across all cloud workloads including infrastructure, containers, serverless functions, and application dependencies.

exampleControls.name: Penetration Testing
exampleControls.summary: Conduct regular penetration testing of cloud environments, coordinating with cloud service providers regarding scope, notification, and acceptable testing methods.

exampleControls.name: Vulnerability Prioritization
exampleControls.summary: Prioritise vulnerability remediation based on exploitability, exposure, asset criticality, and threat intelligence, using risk-based scoring rather than CVSS alone.

controlDomains.name: Universal Endpoint Management
controlDomains.description: Controls for managing and securing endpoints that access cloud services, including mobile devices, laptops, workstations, and IoT devices. Covers device registration, compliance enforcement, and remote management.

exampleControls.name: Endpoint Device Policy
exampleControls.summary: Establish and maintain an endpoint management policy defining device registration, compliance requirements, and security configurations for all devices accessing cloud services.

exampleControls.name: Anti-Malware Detection and Prevention
exampleControls.summary: Implement endpoint protection on all devices accessing cloud services, including next-generation anti-malware, behavioural detection, and automated response capabilities.

exampleControls.name: Remote Wipe
exampleControls.summary: Implement the capability to remotely wipe cloud data from endpoint devices upon loss, theft, or employee termination.

exampleControls.name: Third-Party Endpoint Security Posture
exampleControls.summary: Assess and verify the security posture of third-party endpoints before granting access to cloud services, implementing conditional access policies based on device compliance.
